sections.heading: Settings Overview
sections.content: The Settings tab (tab_settings.py) is the central configuration panel for NEXUS. All values are persisted immediately to settings.json in the project root via save_config() whenever you click Save. The engine reads CONFIG on startup and on each frame — most parameters take effect on the next engine start, while some (like trim and smoothness) are hot-applied in real time via the Dashboard sliders.

sections.heading: Camera Configuration
rows: OpenCV VideoCapture index for the left (primary) camera. Used for AI inference and as the reference frame for homography.
OpenCV VideoCapture index for the right (stereo) camera. Used only for depth estimation via disparity.
Camera capture width in pixels. Affects AI accuracy (higher = better), FPS (higher = slower), and VRAM usage.
Camera capture height in pixels. Should match the camera's native aspect ratio for best results.

sections.heading: Finding the Right Camera Index
sections.content: Camera indices are assigned by the OS and can vary after reboots or USB reconnects. Use this script to discover all available camera indices:
sections.code:
import cv2

print("Scanning camera indices 0–9...")
for i in range(10):
    cap = cv2.VideoCapture(i)
    if cap.isOpened():
        ret, frame = cap.read()
        shape = frame.shape if ret else 'no frame'
        print(f"  Index {i}: ✅ Available — {shape}")
        cap.release()
    else:
        print(f"  Index {i}: ❌ Not available")

# Example output:
# Index 0: ✅ Available — (480, 640, 3)   ← Built-in laptop cam
# Index 1: ✅ Available — (720, 1280, 3)  ← Left USB camera
# Index 4: ✅ Available — (720, 1280, 3)  ← Right USB camera

sections.heading: AI Model Configuration
rows: Relative path from project root to the YOLO model file. Supports .engine (TensorRT — fast) and .pt (PyTorch — portable but slower).
Minimum AI detection confidence score (0.1–0.95). Detections below this threshold are discarded. Lower = more detections but more false positives.
Minimum IoU overlap (0.1–0.9) to maintain a locked target across frames. Lower = easier to maintain lock but may drift to nearby people.
Maximum vertical pixel jump (in pixels) allowed between frames. Jumps larger than this reset the lock. Prevents locking on flickering detections.

sections.heading: Serial / Hardware Configuration
rows: COM port for the ESP32 DevKit. Find this in Device Manager → Ports (COM & LPT) → USB-SERIAL CH340 (COMx).
Serial communication speed. Must match the firmware setting in nexus_guardian_v2.2.ino. Do not change unless you also modify the firmware.
Pan angle (°) sent to servos on engine startup and when safety timeout triggers. 90° = center position.
Tilt angle (°) sent to servos on engine startup and safety timeout. 150° = slightly downward pointing — safe resting angle.
Seconds with no target detection before the system returns to home position and disables the laser. Prevents indefinite aiming at last known position.

sections.heading: Calibration File Paths
rows: Path to the stereo camera calibration .npz archive. Used by the engine for stereo rectification and depth estimation.
Path to the laser homography matrix .npz archive. Used to convert pixel coordinates to servo pan/tilt angles.

sections.heading: Calibration File Tips
sections.content: Both calibration files are optional — the engine degrades gracefully if they are missing:
list: If both files are present → ✅ FULLY CALIBRATED mode: full depth estimation + accurate servo targeting
If only STEREO_FILE is present → ⚠️ STEREO ONLY: depth works but servo angles are raw pixel estimates
If neither file is present → ⚠️ UNCALIBRATED RAW: no depth, approximate targeting only
Use the PY CALIBRATION tab to generate stereo_calibration_py.npz
Use the LASER ALIGNMENT tab to generate laser_alignment.npz
Optionally use DATA BRIDGE to convert a MATLAB .mat calibration to stereo_calibration_bridge.npz

sections.heading: Targeting Fine-Tuning Parameters
rows: Pixels below shoulder midpoint to place the final aim dot. 0=shoulders, ~50=chest, ~100=stomach.
EMA smoothing on servo output. 0=raw snap, 100=ultra-smooth but slow. Best anti-jitter: 40–60.
Kalman filter intensity on AI detections. 0=trust raw detections fully, 100=heavy smoothing.
EMA alpha for stereo depth smoothing. 0.2 = 20% new reading blended with 80% previous depth.
Global pan degree offset added to every servo command after homography. Corrects small systematic misalignment.
Global tilt degree offset added to every servo command after homography.

sections.heading: How save_config() Works
sections.content: Every Settings change is immediately written to disk. The engine reads CONFIG dynamically — changes to trim and smoothness take effect on the next frame without restart:
sections.code:
# From config.py
import json, os

BASE_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
SETTINGS_FILE = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, 'settings.json')

CONFIG = {}  # Global dict used by all modules

def load_config() -> dict:
    """Load settings.json. Creates defaults if file is missing."""
    if not os.path.exists(SETTINGS_FILE):
        CONFIG.update(DEFAULT_CONFIG)
        save_config(CONFIG)
        return CONFIG
    with open(SETTINGS_FILE, 'r') as f:
        CONFIG.update(json.load(f))
    return CONFIG

def save_config(cfg: dict) -> None:
    """Write the current CONFIG dict to settings.json."""
    with open(SETTINGS_FILE, 'w') as f:
        json.dump(cfg, f, indent=2)

# Example: update a value from any module
from config import CONFIG, save_config
CONFIG['SERIAL_PORT'] = 'COM5'
save_config(CONFIG)  # Immediately written to settings.json

sections.heading: Reset to Defaults
sections.content: If Settings get into a broken state, delete settings.json from the project root and restart NEXUS. The file is auto-regenerated with all defaults:
sections.code:
# Windows CMD:
del settings.json
python main.py

# PowerShell:
Remove-Item settings.json
python main.py

# After restart: settings.json is auto-created with safe defaults
# You only need to re-enter: CAM_L_IDX, CAM_R_IDX, SERIAL_PORT

sections.heading: Settings After First Launch
sections.alertContent:
Three settings MUST be configured before starting the engine:
1. CAM_L_IDX — left camera USB index (use the camera diagnostic script to find it)
2. CAM_R_IDX — right camera USB index (must be different from left)
3. SERIAL_PORT — ESP32 COM port from Device Manager

The engine will fail to open cameras or connect to hardware if these are wrong.
